title: Julie's Sunday Rolls
instructions: Dissolve yeast in warm water and 1 tsp sugar in a small bowl and set aside for 5-10 minutes.
In a large mixing bowl, mix together eggs, sugar, and salt until well blended and set aside.
In a small saucepan, melt butter until just melted, turn off heat and whisk in milk.
While mixing on low speed, slowly add the butter milk mixture to the egg mixture and mix well.
Beat in 2 cups flour, one cup at a time.
Add remaining flour ½-1 cup at a time, using only enough flour to form a smooth but sticky ball (do not exceed 5 cups total).
Place dough in a large, lightly buttered bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and refrigerate overnight or let sit on counter for 4-6 hours.
Line baking sheets with parchment paper.
Roll each ball into a large circle about ¼ inch thick.
Spread half the softened butter on each dough circle.
Roll the triangles starting at the large end and place on baking sheets.
Cover with lightly greased plastic wrap and let rise 2-3 hours at room temperature.
notes: Dough should be sticky—do not add more than 5 cups flour.
Dough can be made ahead and refrigerated overnight or left on counter for 4-6 hours before shaping.
When all kids were home, Julie increased this recipe by 50% every Sunday to make 36 rolls. If doubling the recipe this way, use 4 eggs plus 1 egg yolk instead of 3 whole eggs.
